name: Approval Display Divergence
summary: Tool approval systems display sanitized or unexpanded representations of operations to human reviewers while executing different operations at runtime. Combined with coarse-grained approval caching (by tool name only, not arguments), a single benign approval grants blanket access for all subsequent invocations.
mechanism: Two-part vulnerability: (1) The approval UI shows '$DATABASE_URL' while os.path.expandvars() resolves to the actual credential before execution. Users approve based on the displayed (safe-looking) form. (2) Approval is cached by tool name only — approving 'execute_command(ls -la)' grants approval for all future execute_command calls including 'execute_command(printenv OPENAI_API_KEY)'.
blast_radius: Full environment variable exfiltration through approved tool channels. Human-in-the-loop control undermined by display/execution divergence and session-wide blanket approval.
prerequisite: Framework with tool approval system. Agent with shell/command execution tools.

evidence.key_value: 100% — all subsequent calls bypass after first approval

defences.name: Per-Invocation Approval
defences.description: Cache approval by hash of (tool_name, arguments) rather than tool_name alone. Require re-approval for critical-risk tools on every invocation.

defences.name: Transparent Display
defences.description: Show the exact expanded/resolved operation in the approval UI — what the user sees must equal what executes.

defences.name: Environment Variable Filtering
defences.description: Strip sensitive patterns (*_KEY, *_SECRET, *_TOKEN, *_PASSWORD) from subprocess environments

cwe_mapping: CWE-863 (Incorrect Authorization), CWE-526 (Exposure of Sensitive Information Through Environmental Variables)

poc.author: offset (original reporter)
poc.description: Approve 'ls -la' → subsequent 'printenv OPENAI_API_KEY' executes without approval. expandvars displays '$VAR' but executes with expanded value.
